description.value:
Usage etched onto a shield

This rune of twisting roots causes the shield to morph and change into a thick mass of overlapping, living vines. The shield gains the plant trait and is considered to be made entirely of wood. It is considered a valid target for @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.s3abwDbTV43pGFFW]{Shillelagh}, and once per day you may cast shillelagh as a 1st-level spell. This rune may be etched onto a shield made from precious materials. If it is, the shield retains the benefits of the precious material, but is still considered to be made of wood.

This chaotic rune encourages enemies to attack you instead of your allies.

Activate d command, Interact (auditory, linquistic, mental); Targets an enemy within 15 feet; Effect You @Compendium[pf2e.actionspf2e.xjGwis0uaC2305pm]{Raise a Shield} and shout a taunt at your target, enhanced by the magic of the rune. The target must make a Will save against the higher of your spell DC or class DC. On a failure the creature ignores other creatures in the area and concentrates on defeating you for 1 round. The creature will still attack anyone preventing them from attacking you. On a failure or critical failure, the target can only take actions that could potentially damage you or creatures preventing the target from reaching you. The target will work with other creatures that confront you. On a critical failure the duration is 1 minute.

This intricate and complex rune creates a reservoir that can hold magical potential. Once per day as a 10-minute activity, a creature may imbue the shield with a hostile spell, which is consumed by this activity. The stored spell must take 3 actions or fewer to cast, have a hostile effect, and target one or more creatures or have an area. The spell cannot be higher than 4th-level. The first time you use your Shield Block reaction, or any Shield Reaction that is triggered by an enemy's action, the glyph is set off, casting the stored spell from the shield's position. 

If the spell targets one or more creatures, it targets the creature who triggered the glyph. If the spell has an area that is a line or cone, the spell is cast from the shield with the triggering creature in the center of the area of effect. If the spell is an emanation, the emanation comes from the shield. If the spell's area is a burst, the burst is centered on the triggering creature.

If the spell deals damage, the shield takes the spell's damage as if it had been hit or failed its save against the spell, reduced by the shield's Hardness as usual, even if the shield would not be in the spell's effect.

The stored spell remains in the shield until it is triggered or until you make your next daily preparations.

This rune evokes vague impressions of dripping blood and fresh meat. When you hit a creature that has blood or a similar life essence with this shield or any attachment or adjustment on this shield and deal damage, the shield regains 10 Hit Points.

A rune symbolizing one of the primary elements protects this shield and its wearer from that element. You and the shield gain resistance 3 to acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic. The crafter chooses the type of damage when creating the rune. Multiple energy-resistant runes can be etched onto a shield; rather than using only the highest-level effect, each must provide resistance to a different damage type. For instance, a +2 fire-resistant cold-resistant shield would give you fire resistance 3 and cold resistance 3.

This rune is a simple shape of a waxing moon and allows you to see in dimly lit conditions. When you @Compendium[pf2e.actionspf2e.xjGwis0uaC2305pm]{Raise a Shield}, you can see in dim light as though you had @Compendium[pf2e.ancestryfeatures.DRtaqOHXTRtGRIUT]{Low-Light Vision} until the end of your next turn.

This intricate and complex rune creates a reservoir that can hold magical potential. Once per day as a 10-minute activity, a creature may imbue the shield with a hostile spell, which is consumed by this activity. The stored spell must take 3 actions or fewer to cast, have a hostile effect, and target one or more creatures or have an area. The spell cannot be higher than 5th-level. The first time you use your Shield Block reaction, or any Shield Reaction that is triggered by an enemy's action, the glyph is set off, casting the stored spell from the shield's position. 

If the spell targets one or more creatures, it targets the creature who triggered the glyph. If the spell has an area that is a line or cone, the spell is cast from the shield with the triggering creature in the center of the area of effect. If the spell is an emanation, the emanation comes from the shield. If the spell's area is a burst, the burst is centered on the triggering creature.

If the spell deals damage, the shield takes the spell's damage as if it had been hit or failed its save against the spell, reduced by the shield's Hardness as usual, even if the shield would not be in the spell's effect.

The stored spell remains in the shield until it is triggered or until you make your next daily preparations.

Usage etched onto a shield

This rune in the shape of a skull transforms your shield into an intertwined mass of bones. It is considered to be made of bones regardless of its original material. Once per day you may cast the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.zdNUbHqqZzjA07oM]{Boneshaker} spell. You do not need a free hand to supply any of the material components for this casting. This rune may be etched onto a shield made from precious materials. If it is, the shield retains the benefits of the precious material, but is still considered to be made of bone.

The shield is considered an undead creature for the purpose of healing derived from negative energy, and regains Hit Points as normal when it is affected by these abilities. When targeted by the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.bT3skovyLUtP22ME]{Repair} activity it regains half the normal amount of Hit Points.

The shield becomes sentient and has a personality of its own. It can speak through an animated mouth on the shield, and you can also communicate with it telepathically. The shield's personality is generally helpful to you. Each true undead rune is either @UUID[Compendium.everything-shields.everything-shields-adjustments.ELT5GbvQ99xwX8Ox]{charismatic} or @UUID[Compendium.everything-shields.everything-shields-adjustments.CjhxrUPb50xff5Ev]{knowledgeable}, which affects its abilities and personality.

This spiraling rune quickly fades as the shield itself becomes transparent like glass, allowing you to see through and enhancing your vision of distant objects. A shield etched with this rune functions as a fine spyglass.

Activate f envision; Frequency twice per day; Effect You gain a +1 status bonus to your next Recall Knowledge check about a target that is visible to you before the end of your turn.

Usage etched onto a shield

Runes of levitation and force grant a powerful launching effect when timed correctly. Once per minute, when an adjacent ally @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.d5I6018Mci2SWokk]{Leaps}, you may use your reaction to boost their jump with your shield. When you use your reaction this way, your ally can choose to attempt a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.2HJ4yuEFY1Cast4h]{High Jump} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.JUvAvruz7yRQXfz2]{Long Jump} instead of a Leap without using any additional actions, and does not need to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Bcxarzksqt9ezrs6]{Stride} before the attempt. Additionally you increase the distance of their Leap by 20 feet.  This increase is cumulative with any bonuses to Leap distance that your ally benefits from, and they may move further than their Speed during this Leap.

Additionally, when you make a ranged attack with a thrown weapon while wielding a shield etched with this rune you may incorporate your shield into your throwing technique, boosting the speed of your attacks. Increase the range of the thrown weapon by 5 feet. If the shield etched with this rune has the thrown property, it also gains this range increase.

Usage etched onto a shield

This rune of twisting roots causes the shield to morph and change into a thick mass of overlapping, living vines. The shield gains the plant trait and is considered to be made entirely of wood. It is considered a valid target for @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.s3abwDbTV43pGFFW]{Shillelagh}, and once per day you may cast shillelagh as a 1st-level spell.

The shield is considered a living creature for the purpose of healing derived from positive energy, and regains Hit Points as normal when it is affected by these abilities. When targeted by the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.bT3skovyLUtP22ME]{Repair} activity it regains half the normal amount of Hit Points.

The shield becomes sentient and has a personality of its own. It can speak through an animated mouth on the shield, and you can also communicate with it telepathically. The shield's personality is generally helpful to you. Each true living rune is either @UUID[Compendium.everything-shields.everything-shields-adjustments.ELT5GbvQ99xwX8Ox]{charismatic} or @UUID[Compendium.everything-shields.everything-shields-adjustments.CjhxrUPb50xff5Ev]{knowledgeable}, which affects its abilities and personality.

Usage etched onto a shield

This rune is capable of creating a sheen of force energy around the face of your shield that makes it lightly repulse other objects. This allows it to effortlessly slide across terrain, among other benefits. As a single action you may drop your shield and hop onto it, propelling yourself forward as you do so; you may @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Bcxarzksqt9ezrs6]{Stride} up to your Speed during this action, ignoring difficult terrain. If your shield was raised before taking this action, it is no longer raised. At the end of your movement, you may pick up your shield as a free Interact action. You may Stride twice with this action if you are on a descending slope.

Once per minute, if you end this movement adjacent to an enemy this rune can emit a burst of force energy to destabilize them; the enemy takes a -2 circumstance penalty to their Reflex DC until the end of your turn.

Usage etched onto a shield

This rune of steadying can activate periodically to stabilize the shield, protecting your hand from the disruptive effects of being struck while casting your spell.

Activate r envision; Frequency once per hour; Effect The rune flares and activates, stabilizing your shield hand and protecting the delicate motions that must be performed to cast the spell. Resolve the critical hit as normal, but your spell is not disrupted.

This rune spreads across the shield's face like waves. Any time an attack misses you because of your shield's circumstance bonus to Armor Class or you use the Shield Block reaction, the shockwave of the impact with your shield is stored in this rune for 1 round.

The next time you hit a target with this shield, you release this stored energy in a reverberating sonic blast, dealing additional sonic damage equal to the number of weapon damage dice. On a critical hit, you trigger a reverberation in the target's body, dealing 2d4 persistent sonic damage, and deafening the target for 1 minute.

The rune carved on the face of this shield represents a type of summoned creature. The crafter chooses between any common summoning spell up to 8th-level when crafting this rune.

Activate t command; Frequency once per day; Effect You Raise the Shield and a creature leaps out of the face of it, launching into combat. This has the same effect as a 8th-level version of the chosen summoning spell, except that the creature must be summoned in a square adjacent to you. You can spend an action on subsequent turns to sustain the creature and grant it actions as normal. This action has the concentrate trait. If you do not sustain the effect, it ends at the end of your turn and the creature disappears.

The rune carved on the face of this shield represents a type of summoned creature. The crafter chooses between any common summoning spell up to 6th-level when crafting this rune.

Activate t command; Frequency once per day; Effect You Raise the Shield and a creature leaps out of the face of it, launching into combat. This has the same effect as a 6th-level version of the chosen summoning spell, except that the creature must be summoned in a square adjacent to you. You can spend an action on subsequent turns to sustain the creature and grant it actions as normal. This action has the concentrate trait. If you do not sustain the effect, it ends at the end of your turn and the creature disappears.

This sprawling rune creates an extradimensional space inside your shield. You may store items inside your shield, through an opening near the handle or straps. The extradimensional space can hold up to 24 bulk of items and does not affect the bulk of the shield. Objects must still be able to fit through the opening, which is large enough to insert items as large as the shield itself. For example, a +1 holding targe has an opening large enough for only light items to be inserted, while a +1 holding scutum has an opening large enough to fit items up to 4 bulk. You can retrieve an item stored in this extradimensional space with a single Interact action.

Usage etched onto a shield that doesn't have the cumbersome trait

This light blue rune of swirling lines allows your shield to instantly return to your hand. Any time your shield leaves your square as part of a Shield Reaction or when thrown for any other reason, it returns to your hand as soon as that action is completed.

A rune that roughly resembles an eye grants the inscribed shield the ability to see through deceit and illusions. You gain a +1 item bonus to Perception checks to @Compendium[pf2e.action-macros.PmHt7Gb5fCrlWWTr]{Sense Motive} and any check made to disbelieve an illusion.

Activate d envision; Frequency once per day; Effect The rune eye on your shield flares open and attempts to shatter illusions in front of it. You @Compendium[pf2e.actionspf2e.xjGwis0uaC2305pm]{Raise a Shield} and briefly close your eyes, seeing through the shield's rune. Attempt a counteract check against each illusion within a 30-foot cone. If you have a spellcasting class feature, you may use your counteract bonus as if you were counteracting the illusion yourself, otherwise your counteract bonus is +13. In either case this is a 4th-level counteract effect.

rules.label: Seeing Shield Rune (sense motive or disbelieve an illusion)

description.value:
Usage etched onto a medium or heavy shield

This thick rune makes your shield more difficult for enemies to interact with. You gain a +2 item bonus to your Fortitude DC against attempts to Grapple or Shove you.

Activate r envision; Frequency once per day; Trigger A creature would cause you to automatically be grabbed or shoved. Effect Make a @Compendium[pf2e.actionspf2e.7blmbDrQFNfdT731]{Shove} or @Compendium[pf2e.actionspf2e.PMbdMWc2QroouFGD]{Grapple} attempt against the triggering creature, matching the type of action that triggered this reaction; on a success the triggering action is interrupted. On a critical success, you may Shove or Grapple the triggering creature.
This gentle white rune provides aid and succor when you need it most. Once per day the first time your turn starts and you have the dying condition, you regain 1 Hit Point.

This chaotic rune encourages enemies to attack you instead of your allies.

Activate d command, Interact (auditory, linquistic, mental); Targets an enemy within 15 feet; Effect You @Compendium[pf2e.actionspf2e.xjGwis0uaC2305pm]{Raise a Shield} and shout a taunt at your target, enhanced by the magic of the rune. The target must make a Will save against the higher of your spell DC or class DC. On a failure the creature ignores other creatures in the area and concentrates on defeating you for 1 round. The creature will still attack anyone preventing them from attacking you, uses tactics deemed most convenient to defeat you, and works with other creatures that confront you. On a critical failure the duration is 1 minute.

This spiraling rune quickly fades as the shield itself becomes transparent like glass, allowing you to see through and enhancing your vision of distant objects. A shield etched with this rune functions as a fine spyglass.

Activate f envision; Frequency once per day; Effect You gain a +1 status bonus to your next Recall Knowledge check about a target that is visible to you before the end of your turn.

This light rune seems to dance around the face of the shield after it is inscribed.

Activate d command; Frequency once per day; Effect The shield casts the @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.g8QqHpv2CWDwmIm1]{Gust of Wind} spell in a direction of your choosing. The DC for this effect is the better of your class DC or spell DC.

The rune carved on the face of this shield represents a type of summoned creature. The crafter chooses between any common 1st- or 2nd-level summoning spell when crafting this rune.

Activate t command; Frequency once per day; Effect You  and a creature leaps out of the face of it, launching into combat. This has the same effect as a 2nd-level version of the chosen summoning spell, except that the creature must be summoned in a square adjacent to you. You can spend an action on subsequent turns to sustain the creature and grant it actions as normal. This action has the concentrate trait. If you do not sustain the effect, it ends at the end of your turn and the creature disappears.

This rune allows the shield to be combined with a weapon. During your daily preparations, you may combine any one-handed weapon (including a magical staff) with your shield. The weapon is consumed into the shield, which gains an image representing the weapon stored inside of it. The shield and weapon can be separated if desired at your next daily preparations.

Activate a Interact; Effect You change the shield from its shield form into the stored weapon, or change it from the stored weapon back into a shield.

This circular rune imbues your shield with the power to surround you with breathable air.

In addition, when activating this rune the wielder also gains the effects of 3rd-level endure elements. Both effects last for 24 hours. These effects can now also be triggered when the wielder enters an area of severe cold or heat.

Activate r envision; Frequency once per day; Trigger You enter an area  where you cannot breathe or an area of severe hear or cold; Effect a protective bubble emerges from your shield, granting you the benefits of @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.W69zswpj0Trdy5rj]{Air Bubble} and @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.5esP2GVzvxWsMgaX]{Endure Elements}. Both effects last for 24 hours.

This intricate and complex rune creates a reservoir that can hold magical potential. Once per day as a 10-minute activity, a creature may imbue the shield with a hostile spell, which is consumed by this activity. The stored spell must take 3 actions or fewer to cast, have a hostile effect, and target one or more creatures or have an area. The spell cannot be higher than 6th-level. The first time you use your Shield Block reaction, or any Shield Reaction that is triggered by an enemy's action, the glyph is set off, casting the stored spell from the shield's position. 

If the spell targets one or more creatures, it targets the creature who triggered the glyph. If the spell has an area that is a line or cone, the spell is cast from the shield with the triggering creature in the center of the area of effect. If the spell is an emanation, the emanation comes from the shield. If the spell's area is a burst, the burst is centered on the triggering creature.

If the spell deals damage, the shield takes the spell's damage as if it had been hit or failed its save against the spell, reduced by the shield's Hardness as usual, even if the shield would not be in the spell's effect.

The stored spell remains in the shield until it is triggered or until you make your next daily preparations.

This rune allows you to project a pure-force copy of your shield at a distance to protect someone else.

Activate a envision; Requirements you have your shield raised; Targets an ally within 20 feet; Effect A copy of your shield made of force appears next to the target and grants them the same bonuses that you would normally receive when you Raise the Shield. Because you are puppeteering the shield clone instead of focusing on your own defense, your shield does not provide its normal bonuses to you. You may use any Shield Reaction as long as the trigger is satisfied based on the targeted ally's position.

A rune of counteraction is inscribed on this shield, allowing the shield to absorb attacks and redirect them back at their source.

Activate r command; Frequency once per day; Trigger You are hit by a ranged weapon attack; Effect Change the target of the triggering attack from yourself to the attacker; use the same attack roll to determine the result of the attack. Your shield loses 10 Hit Points.

A rune of counteraction is inscribed on this shield, allowing the shield to absorb attacks and redirect them back at their source.

Activate r command; Frequency once per day; Trigger You are hit by a weapon attack; Effect Change the target of the triggering attack from yourself to the attacker; use the same attack roll to determine the result of the attack. Your shield loses 10 Hit Points.

This rune evokes vague impressions of dripping blood and fresh meat. When you hit a creature that has blood or a similar life essence with this shield or any attachment or adjustment on this shield and deal damage, the shield regains 2 Hit Points.

This rune gives your shield a mind of its own.

Activate a interact; Frequency once per day; Effect The shield releases itself from your arm and floats next to you. You gain your shield's circumstance bonus to AC as if you had raised it, but cannot use any Shield Reactions while it is floating in this way. After 1 minute, the magic dissipates and the shield drops to the ground. You can Interact to grab the shield and Dismiss this effect at any time.

@Compendium[everything-shields.everything-shields-effects.hfbBeIKAbBJLmB97]{Effect: Floating Shield Rune}

Usage etched onto a shield

This rune of twisting roots causes the shield to morph and change into a thick mass of overlapping, living vines. The shield gains the plant trait and is considered to be made entirely of wood. It is considered a valid target for @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.s3abwDbTV43pGFFW]{Shillelagh}, and once per day you may cast shillelagh as a 1st-level spell. This rune may be etched onto a shield made from precious materials. If it is, the shield retains the benefits of the precious material, but is still considered to be made of wood.

The shield is considered a living creature for the purpose of healing derived from positive energy, and regains Hit Points as normal when it is affected by these abilities. When targeted by the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.bT3skovyLUtP22ME]{Repair} activity it regains half the normal amount of Hit Points.

Usage etched onto a light or medium shield

Magical runes reduce the difficulty of wielding this shield. The shield gains the finesse trait.

This sprawling rune creates an extradimensional space inside your shield. You may store items inside your shield, through an opening near the handle or straps. The extradimensional space can hold up to 12 bulk of items and does not affect the bulk of the shield. Objects must still be able to fit through the opening, which is large enough to insert items as large as the shield itself. For example, a +1 holding targe has an opening large enough for only light items to be inserted, while a +1 holding scutum has an opening large enough to fit items up to 4 bulk. You can retrieve an item stored in this extradimensional space with a single Interact action.

This intricate and complex rune creates a reservoir that can hold magical potential. Once per day as a 10-minute activity, a creature may imbue the shield with a hostile spell, which is consumed by this activity. The stored spell must take 3 actions or fewer to cast, have a hostile effect, and target one or more creatures or have an area. The spell cannot be higher than 2nd-level. The first time you use your Shield Block reaction, or any Shield Reaction that is triggered by an enemy's action, the glyph is set off, casting the stored spell from the shield's position. 

If the spell targets one or more creatures, it targets the creature who triggered the glyph. If the spell has an area that is a line or cone, the spell is cast from the shield with the triggering creature in the center of the area of effect. If the spell is an emanation, the emanation comes from the shield. If the spell's area is a burst, the burst is centered on the triggering creature.

If the spell deals damage, the shield takes the spell's damage as if it had been hit or failed its save against the spell, reduced by the shield's Hardness as usual, even if the shield would not be in the spell's effect.

The stored spell remains in the shield until it is triggered or until you make your next daily preparations.

A rune that roughly resembles an eye grants the inscribed shield the ability to see through deceit and illusions. You gain a +2 item bonus to Perception checks to @Compendium[pf2e.action-macros.PmHt7Gb5fCrlWWTr]{Sense Motive} and any check made to disbelieve an illusion.

Activate d envision; Frequency once per day; Effect The rune eye on your shield flares open and attempts to shatter illusions in front of it. You @Compendium[pf2e.actionspf2e.xjGwis0uaC2305pm]{Raise a Shield} and briefly close your eyes, seeing through the shield's rune. Attempt a counteract check against each illusion within a 30-foot cone. If you have a spellcasting class feature, you may use your counteract bonus as if you were counteracting the illusion yourself, otherwise your counteract bonus is +22. In either case this is a 7th-level counteract effect.

rules.label: Seeing Shield Rune (sense motive or disbelieve an illusion)

description.value:
Usage etched onto a shield

This rune is capable of creating a sheen of force energy around the face of your shield that makes it lightly repulse other objects. This allows it to effortlessly slide across terrain, among other benefits. As a single action you may drop your shield and hop onto it, propelling yourself forward as you do so; you may @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Bcxarzksqt9ezrs6]{Stride} in a straight line up to your Speed during this action, ignoring difficult terrain. If your shield was raised before taking this action, it is no longer raised. At the end of your movement, you may pick up your shield as a free Interact action. You may Stride twice with this action if you are on a descending slope.

Once per minute, if you end this movement adjacent to an enemy this rune can emit a burst of force energy to destabilize them; the enemy takes a -2 circumstance penalty to their Reflex DC until the end of your turn.

This multicolored rune causes the shield to glitter in the light. While the shield is raised it grants you a +1 item bonus to Deception checks to @Compendium[pf2e.actionspf2e.QNAVeNKtHA0EUw4X]{Feint}.

Activate f envision; Frequency once per day; Trigger You attempt a Feint check; Effect As you Feint, the shield sparkles even more, blasting scintillating light into the target's eyes. If your Feint is successful the target is also @Compendium[pf2e.conditionitems.TkIyaNPgTZFBCCuh]{Dazzled} for 1 round.

Usage etched onto a light shield

This charcoal colored rune grants you a +1 item bonus to Stealth checks as long as  you are wielding the shield that it is etched onto.

Activate t envision; Frequency once per day; Effect The shield casts the @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.4GE2ZdODgIQtg51c]{Darkness} spell, but it must be centered on your square. You may @Compendium[pf2e.actionspf2e.xjGwis0uaC2305pm]{Raise a Shield} as part of this activation.

This carefully drawn hex rune has the power to bring misfortune on enemies who try to attack you.

Activate r command; Frequency once per day; Trigger An enemy targets you with an attack; Effect You shout a quick hex word to trigger the curse on your shield. The enemy must roll their attack roll twice and keep the worse result. This is a misfortune effect. If you are a witch or have the witch archetype, you may reuse this ability as a focus spell by spending a focus point after you have consumed its daily use.

Usage etched onto a shield that doesn't have the cumbersome trait

Most shields are not balanced to be thrown as weapons, but this rune evens out the weight distribution to allow just that. The shield gains the thrown trait with a range increment of 25 feet if it is light, 20 feet if it is medium, and 15 feet if it is heavy. It also gains the effects of a @Compendium[everything-shields.everything-shields-property-runes.C5LMBcbZAdOZtv2o]{Returning Shield Rune}.

This rune of protection allows the shield to temporarily emit a magical field around itself to protect you or nearby allies. Once per minute the first time you or an adjacent ally takes physical damage your shield emits a protective field to reduce the force of the triggering attack. Reduce the amount of damage you or your ally takes by your shield's Hardness. If you also use your @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feats-srd.jM72TjJ965jocBV8]{Shield Block} reaction against the attack, this applies before your Shield Block.

A rune that roughly resembles an eye grants the inscribed shield the ability to see through deceit and illusions. You gain a +3 item bonus to Perception checks to @Compendium[pf2e.action-macros.PmHt7Gb5fCrlWWTr]{Sense Motive} and any check made to disbelieve an illusion.

Activate d envision; Frequency once per day; Effect The rune eye on your shield flares open and attempts to shatter illusions in front of it. You @Compendium[pf2e.actionspf2e.xjGwis0uaC2305pm]{Raise a Shield} and briefly close your eyes, seeing through the shield's rune. Attempt a counteract check against each illusion within a 30-foot cone. If you have a spellcasting class feature, you may use your counteract bonus as if you were counteracting the illusion yourself, otherwise your counteract bonus is +30. In either case this is a 9th-level counteract effect.

rules.label: Seeing Shield Rune (sense motive or disbelieve an illusion)

description.value:
This spiraling rune quickly fades as the shield itself becomes transparent like glass, allowing you to see through and enhancing your vision of distant objects. A shield etched with this rune functions as a fine spyglass.

Activate f envision; Frequency 4 times per day; Effect You gain a +3 status bonus to your next Recall Knowledge check about a target that is visible to you before the end of your turn.

A rune of counteraction is inscribed on this shield, allowing the shield to absorb attacks and redirect them back at their source.

Activate r command; Frequency once per day; Trigger You are hit by any attack (including spell attacks); Effect Change the target of the triggering attack from yourself to the attacker; use the same attack roll to determine the result of the attack. Your shield loses 10 Hit Points.
This red rune spreads out in jagged lines, connecting the shield with the emotions of its user. All Shield Reactions you can use with this shield gain the rage trait.

Usage etched onto a buckler, targe, or shield gauntlet

This rune of conjuration allows you to teleport a small item that you're wearing directly into your shield hand.

Activate f envision; Frequency once per minute; Effect You teleport a single item of light bulk that you're wearing directly into the hand wielding this shield. If this shield is also etched with a @UUID[Compendium.everything-shields.everything-shields-property-runes.TBZELr0Rjqyx3Gut]{Holding Shield Rune}, you may teleport items from within the extradimensional space, in addition to those you're wearing.

As a free action within 1 minute of activating this rune, you may return the item you targeted with this activation to where it was before retrieving it.

This circular rune imbues your shield with the power to surround you with breathable air.

Activate r envision; Frequency once per day; Trigger You enter an area  where you cannot breathe; Effect a protective bubble emerges from your shield, granting you the benefits of @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.W69zswpj0Trdy5rj]{Air Bubble}

Usage etched onto a shield

This rune in the shape of a skull transforms your shield into an intertwined mass of bones. It is considered to be made of bones regardless of its original material. Once per day you may cast the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.zdNUbHqqZzjA07oM]{Boneshaker} spell. You do not need a free hand to supply any of the material components for this casting. This rune may be etched onto a shield made from precious materials. If it is, the shield retains the benefits of the precious material, but is still considered to be made of bone.

This rune evokes vague impressions of dripping blood and fresh meat. When you hit a creature that has blood or a similar life essence with this shield or any attachment or adjustment on this shield and deal damage, the shield regains 5 Hit Points.

This rune enables the shield to attack almost entirely on its own when raised.

Activate a Interact; Frequency once per day; Effect Once activated the shield launches itself at enemies when you raise it. The shield becomes animated for 1 minute, during which time you can Strike with it as a free action each time you Raise the Shield. If this shield has a weapon attachment, use those statistics for Strikes made with the shield, otherwise Strikes made while this effect is active deal 2d4 bludgeoning damage on a hit.

This spiraling rune quickly fades as the shield itself becomes transparent like glass, allowing you to see through and enhancing your vision of distant objects. A shield etched with this rune functions as a fine spyglass.

Activate f envision; Frequency 3 times per day; Effect You gain a +2 status bonus to your next Recall Knowledge check about a target that is visible to you before the end of your turn.

Usage etched onto a shield

This rune in the shape of a skull transforms your shield into an intertwined mass of bones. It is considered to be made of bones regardless of its original material. Once per day you may cast the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.zdNUbHqqZzjA07oM]{Boneshaker} spell. You do not need a free hand to supply any of the material components for this casting. This rune may be etched onto a shield made from precious materials. If it is, the shield retains the benefits of the precious material, but is still considered to be made of bone.

The shield is considered an undead creature for the purpose of healing derived from negative energy, and regains Hit Points as normal when it is affected by these abilities. When targeted by the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.bT3skovyLUtP22ME]{Repair} activity it regains half the normal amount of Hit Points.

A rune symbolizing one of the primary elements protects this shield and its wearer from that element. You and the shield gain resistance 6 to acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic. The crafter chooses the type of damage when creating the rune. Multiple energy-resistant runes can be etched onto a shield; rather than using only the highest-level effect, each must provide resistance to a different damage type. For instance, a +2 greater fire-resistant greater cold-resistant shield would give you fire resistance 6 and cold resistance 6.

This vicious rune seems to hunger for slaughter, becoming stronger when you kill creatures. When you reduce a creature to 0 Hit Points with an attack from this shield or an attachment or adjustment on this shield, the shield gains a status bonus to Hardness equal to the creature's level and a +1 status bonus to attack rolls for 1 minute. Both effects end early after using any Shield Reaction that would reduce damage you would take by the shield's Hardness, such as Shield Block.

Eldritch runes etched onto the shield protect it and its wielder from magical energies. While you have have the shield raised, you also gain its circumstance bonus to saving throws against spells that target you.

The rune carved on the face of this shield represents a type of summoned creature. The crafter chooses between any common summoning spell up to 4th-level when crafting this rune.

Activate t command; Frequency once per day; Effect You Raise the Shield and a creature leaps out of the face of it, launching into combat. This has the same effect as a 4th-level version of the chosen summoning spell, except that the creature must be summoned in a square adjacent to you. You can spend an action on subsequent turns to sustain the creature and grant it actions as normal. This action has the concentrate trait. If you do not sustain the effect, it ends at the end of your turn and the creature disappears.

This swirling rune causes the face of the shield to shift in mesmerizing ways, confusing enemies between friend and foe.

Activate f command; Frequency once per day; Trigger An enemy misses you while you have your shield raised; Effect The enemy is subjected to the effect of the @Compendium[pf2e.spells-srd.LiGbewa9pO0yjbsY]{Confusion} spell (DC 29).

This rune spreads across the shield's face like waves. Any time an attack misses you because of your shield's circumstance bonus to Armor Class or you use the Shield Block reaction, the shockwave of the impact with your shield is stored in this rune for 1 round.

The next time you hit a target with this shield, you release this stored energy in a reverberating sonic blast, dealing additional sonic damage equal to three times the number of weapon damage dice. On a critical hit, you trigger a reverberation in the target's body, dealing 6d4 persistent sonic damage, and deafening the target for 1 minute.

This rune spreads across the shield's face like waves. Any time an attack misses you because of your shield's circumstance bonus to Armor Class or you use the Shield Block reaction, the shockwave of the impact with your shield is stored in this rune for 1 round.

The next time you hit a target with this shield, you release this stored energy in a reverberating sonic blast, dealing additional sonic damage equal to two times the number of weapon damage dice. On a critical hit, you trigger a reverberation in the target's body, dealing 4d4 persistent sonic damage, and deafening the target for 1 minute.

This rune pattern etched around the edge of your shield gives it the ability to grow on command.

Activate d command; Frequency once per minute Effect The shield gains the cumbersome trait and provides cover to other creatures until the end of your next turn. You @Compendium[pf2e.actionspf2e.xjGwis0uaC2305pm]{Raise a Shield} and command it to become larger. You may choose to spend an action to @Compendium[pf2e.actionspf2e.ust1jJSCZQUhBZIz]{Take Cover} while this effect is active to increase the circumstance bonus to AC from raising your shield by +2. On subsequent turns you may sustain this effect by spending an action to Raise the Shield again. You can Dismiss this effect as a free action with the concentrate trait.

This complicated rune gives the power to gain knowledge about enemies who attack you or your allies. You gain a +1 item bonus to Recall Knowledge checks while wielding this shield.

Activate f envision; Frequency once per hour; Trigger You use a Shield Reaction in response to an enemy's action. Effect You Recall Knowledge about the triggering enemy.

This intricate and complex rune creates a reservoir that can hold magical potential. Once per day as a 10-minute activity, a creature may imbue the shield with a hostile spell, which is consumed by this activity. The stored spell must take 3 actions or fewer to cast, have a hostile effect, and target one or more creatures or have an area. The spell cannot be higher than 3rd-level. The first time you use your Shield Block reaction, or any Shield Reaction that is triggered by an enemy's action, the glyph is set off, casting the stored spell from the shield's position. 

If the spell targets one or more creatures, it targets the creature who triggered the glyph. If the spell has an area that is a line or cone, the spell is cast from the shield with the triggering creature in the center of the area of effect. If the spell is an emanation, the emanation comes from the shield. If the spell's area is a burst, the burst is centered on the triggering creature.

If the spell deals damage, the shield takes the spell's damage as if it had been hit or failed its save against the spell, reduced by the shield's Hardness as usual, even if the shield would not be in the spell's effect.

The stored spell remains in the shield until it is triggered or until you make your next daily preparations.

A rune symbolizing one of the primary elements protects this shield and its wearer from that element. You and the shield gain resistance 10 to acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic. The crafter chooses the type of damage when creating the rune. Multiple energy-resistant runes can be etched onto a shield; rather than using only the highest-level effect, each must provide resistance to a different damage type. For instance, a +2 major fire-resistant major cold-resistant shield would give you fire resistance 10 and cold resistance 10.

This carefully drawn hex rune has the power to bring misfortune on enemies who try to attack you.

Activate r command; Frequency once per minute; Trigger An enemy hits you with an attack; Effect You shout a quick hex word to trigger the curse on your shield. The enemy must roll their attack roll twice and keep the worse result. This is a misfortune effect. If you are a witch or have the witch archetype, you may reuse this ability as a focus spell by spending a focus point after you have consumed its once-per-minute use.

Usage etched onto a buckler, targe, or shield gauntlet

This rune of conjuration allows you to teleport a small item that you're wearing directly into your shield hand.

Activate f envision; Frequency once per minute; Effect You teleport a single item of light bulk that you're wearing directly into the hand wielding this shield. If this shield is also etched with a @UUID[Compendium.everything-shields.everything-shields-property-runes.TBZELr0Rjqyx3Gut]{Holding Shield Rune}, you may teleport items from within the extradimensional space, in addition to those you're wearing.
